author: mark
text:
plugin: native participant protocol [bl-8b71]

Lets a plugin opt into structured propose/conflict negotiation by
shipping `describe` and `propose` subcommands. describe declares the
plugin's projection (owned canonical fields plus external_prefix
slices) and event subscriptions; propose returns either an `ok`
result with the plugin's projection of the post-event Task or a
`conflict` report that triggers SPEC §7 retry. The plugin manages its
own remote-state memory between attempts so disjoint-projection
composability survives mixed configs.

The dispatcher routes per plugin: describe success → native protocol,
describe failure → legacy shim. Both feed a unified PushContribution
stream so the SPEC §10 commit-policy planner sequences mixed configs
in stable subscription order. Apply is projection-aware so two
disjoint plugins do not clobber each other's external slices.
